mechanism_narrative: A lower federal funds rate reduces the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly increasing the present value of S&P 500 cash flows and generating positive forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical correlation with FEDFUNDS levels over a 5-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending economic contraction, which reduces corporate profit expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns remain positive in > 55% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.5pp over 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, which directly boosts corporate revenue growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when either series deviates from its 12-month moving average by less than 1 standard deviation, or interaction term IC < 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the industrial production index reflects expanding manufacturing and utility output, which generates positive short-term market momentum and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in > 60% of months where INDPRO increases, or the signal loses predictive power during supply-constrained regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated consumer price inflation combined with a low federal funds rate compresses real interest rates and erodes purchasing power, triggering monetary policy tightening expectations that produce negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns are positive in > 50% of observations when CPIAUCSL exceeds 230 and FEDFUNDS remains below 1.0%, or the interaction effect is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls indicate expanding labor market participation and higher aggregate consumer spending, which supports corporate earnings growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero correlation with 20-day forward returns over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC remains below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive quarters of negative real GDP growth confirm a technical recession, which severely contracts corporate profit margins and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are positive in > 45% of confirmed recession quarters, or the signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in industrial production and decreases in the unemployment rate signal a synchronized economic upturn, which boosts market sentiment and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal produces negative 5-day returns in > 55% of aligned observations, or the interaction term fails to improve out-of-sample prediction accuracy.
